event: history of intermittent subjective fever of no specific periodicity
no history of loss of consciousness
pulse rate 103 beats/min in sinus rhythm
peripheral blood smear positive for malaria
admitted to the intensive care unit
contrast-enhanced CT scan of the abdomen performed
hypoechoic nodular cystic area on ultrasound
splenic laceration on the superolateral aspect
attributed to severe malaria due to P. falciparum
met the four criteria for spontaneous splenic rupture by Orloff and Peskin
spleen enlarged and very firm (intra-operative finding)
long laceration on the upper pole involving the capsule
histopathology revealed slight enlargement but otherwise grossly and microscopically normal
no other disease affecting the spleen
scheduled clinic visits for post-splenectomy care
